en.extract:
The black pitohui (Melanorectes nigrescens) is a species of bird in the monotypic genus of Melanorectes in the family Pachycephalidae.
It is found throughout the highlands of New Guinea.
Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.


Taxonomy and systematics
The black pitohui was originally described in the genus Rectes (a synonym for the genus Pitohui) and re-classified to Melanorectes in 2013. Alternate names include black whistler and dusky pitohui.


Subspecies
The following six subspecies are recognized:

M. n. nigrescens – (Schlegel, 1871): Found in northwestern New Guinea
M. n. wandamensis – (Hartert, 1930): Found in the Wandammen Peninsula (western New Guinea)
M. n. meeki – (Rothschild & Hartert, 1913): Originally described as a separate species in the genus Pitohui. Found in west-central New Guinea
M. n. buergersi – (Stresemann, 1922): Found in northern and east-central New Guinea
M. n. harterti – Reichenow, 1911: Originally described as a separate species. Found in northeastern New Guinea
M. n. schistaceus – (Reichenow, 1900): Found in southeastern New Guinea


References

The '''black pitohui''' ('''''Melanorectes nigrescens''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the [[monotypic]] genus of '''''Melanorectes''''' in the family [[Pachycephalidae]].
It is found throughout the highlands of [[New Guinea]].
Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s.

==Taxonomy and systematics==
The black pitohui was originally described in the genus ''Rectes'' (a synonym for the genus ''[[Pitohui (genus)|Pitohui]]'') and re-classified to ''Melanorectes'' in 2013.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/archive-3-1-to-3-5/taxonomy-3-1-3-5/|title=Taxonomy 3.1-3.5 « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-01-29}}</ref> Alternate names include '''black whistler''' and '''dusky pitohui'''.

=== Subspecies ===
The following six [[subspecies]] are recognized:<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/whistlers/|title=Whiteheads, sitellas & whistlers « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-01-31}}</ref>
* ''M. n. nigrescens'' – <small>([[Hermann Schlegel|Schlegel]], 1871)</small>: Found in northwestern New Guinea
* ''M. n. wandamensis'' – <small>([[Ernst Hartert|Hartert]], 1930)</small>: Found in the [[Wandammen Peninsula]] (western New Guinea)
* ''M. n. meeki'' – <small>([[Walter Rothschild, 2nd Baron Rothschild|Rothschild]] & [[Ernst Hartert|Hartert]], 1913)</small>: Originally described as a separate species in the genus ''[[Pitohui (genus)|Pitohui]]''. Found in west-central New Guinea
* ''M. n. buergersi'' – <small>([[Erwin Stresemann|Stresemann]], 1922)</small>: Found in northern and east-central New Guinea
* ''M. n. harterti'' – <small>[[Reichenow]], 1911</small>: Originally described as a separate species. Found in northeastern New Guinea
* ''M. n. schistaceus'' – <small>(Reichenow, 1900)</small>: Found in southeastern New Guinea
